record.field: Stack Auth is an open-source authentication and user-management platform, a self-hostable alternative to Auth0 and Clerk.

Stack Auth is an open-source authentication and user-management platform, a self-hostable alternative to Auth0 and Clerk.

Stack Auth gives developers a complete login, signup and user-management stack that drops into web apps in minutes. It ships pre-built UI components alongside a headless SDK and REST API, so teams can use ready-made flows or build fully custom front- and backends. Originally an authentication tool, the project is expanding into a broader user-infrastructure platform (payments, analytics, webhooks, secrets) while keeping its auth core, and you can export your data and self-host for free at any time.

Key features
  • Authentication with passwords, passkeys, OAuth/SSO, magic links and 2FA
  • Pre-built React/Next.js components plus a headless SDK and REST API
  • B2B organizations and teams with email invitations and role-based permission trees
  • Managed JWTs and access tokens, with user impersonation for support
  • Signed, retried webhooks to react to user events in real time
  • Full self-hosting via Docker (Postgres + ClickHouse) with no vendor lock-in
Best for

Developers and SaaS teams that want a modern, customizable auth and user-management layer without building it from scratch or getting locked into a proprietary identity vendor.

services: stack-auth-db: image: postgres:17 environment: - POSTGRES_USER={{ postgres_user }} - POSTGRES_PASSWORD={{ postgres_password }} - POSTGRES_DB={{ postgres_db }} volumes: - ./stack_auth_db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready - -d - db_prod interval: 10s timeout: 60s retries: 5 start_period: 80s container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_stack_auth_db' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal stack-auth: image: stackauth/server:latest container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' environment: - POSTGRES_USER={{ postgres_user }} - POSTGRES_PASSWORD={{ postgres_password }} - POSTGRES_DB={{ postgres_db }} - NEXT_PUBLIC_STACK_API_URL={{ next_public_stack_api_url }} - NEXT_PUBLIC_STACK_DASHBOARD_URL={{ next_public_stack_dashboard_url }} - STACK_DATABASE_CONNECTION_STRING={{ stack_database_connection_string }} - STACK_DIRECT_DATABASE_CONNECTION_STRING={{ stack_direct_database_connection_string }} - STACK_SERVER_SECRET={{ stack_server_secret }} - STACK_SEED_INTERNAL_PROJECT_ALLOW_LOCALHOST={{ stack_seed_internal_project_allow_localhost }} - STACK_SEED_INTERNAL_PROJECT_SIGN_UP_ENABLED={{ stack_seed_internal_project_sign_up_enabled }} - STACK_RUN_MIGRATIONS={{ stack_run_migrations }} - STACK_RUN_SEED_SCRIPT={{ stack_run_seed_script }} - STACK_EMAIL_HOST=${STACK_EMAIL_HOST} depends_on: stack-auth-db: condition: service_healthy restart: unless-stopped ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8102' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T stack-auth-db sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d stack-auth-db for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T stack-auth-db sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T stack-auth-db sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T stack-auth-db sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
